blurb: Google laid out a database strategy built on inexact, semantic queries and AI embedded across its data platforms, with a product exec predicting that humans will no longer directly operate data platforms within three to five years. The pitch points enterprise buyers toward agent-driven querying over BigQuery and AlloyDB, reframing the database from a tool analysts run to infrastructure that AI agents interrogate on their behalf.

title: EDB converges analytics on Postgres to support AI agents
blurb: EDB added converged analytics to EDB Postgres AI, keeping Postgres as source of truth via Iceberg to feed AI agents fresh data.

title: IBM Getting Ready to Scale Quantum Computing
blurb: IBM is moving to commercialize quantum computing this year, building the manufacturing, software, and partner infrastructure to turn its quantum program from a research effort into a scalable business. The shift signals IBM's bet that enterprise customers will pay for quantum access through its cloud, positioning it against Google and emerging quantum startups as the technology approaches practical workloads.

title: Snyk launches Evo Agentic Development Security to police AI coding agents
blurb: Snyk launched Evo Agentic Development Security, a layer of its AI security platform that governs the tools, runtime actions, and generated code of autonomous coding agents from inside the agent workflow rather than scanning output afterward. Drawing on telemetry from nearly 9,700 developer environments where over half run MCP servers, Snyk pitches Evo ADS as inventory and policy enforcement for the agent toolchain that conventional code scanners never covered.

title: Microsoft outs Windows 11 KB5095093 with long list of new features
blurb: Microsoft released KB5095093, a June 2026 preview update for Windows 11 24H2 and 25H2, adding point-in-time restore, a calendar-based 35-day update pause, a quieter Widgets experience, and accessibility additions like screen tint and Magnifier zoom controls. The features roll out gradually, so IT admins should expect staggered availability across fleets and plan validation around the optional C-release rather than the mandatory monthly patch.

title: Anthropic’s Claude Tag aims to turn workplace AI from a personal assistant into a teammate
blurb: Anthropic launched Claude Tag, a Slack channel-based AI collaborator for its Enterprise and Team customers that retains context across conversations, completes tasks autonomously with a reviewable log, and schedules its own follow-up work. Replacing the single-user Claude in Slack, Tag adds an ambient mode that proactively surfaces updates from connected tools and channels, targeting reduced coordination overhead across engineering and business teams.

title: Microsoft adds new AI study and teaching tools for free to Microsoft 365 Education
blurb: Microsoft added Copilot Notebooks, Study and Learn agents, and a Teach agent for curriculum planning to the base Microsoft 365 Education suite at no extra cost, after previously gating Copilot for education behind an $18-per-user monthly tier. The free bundle pressures Google's NotebookLM and Classroom in the K-12 and higher-ed market while deepening district lock-in, with Learning Zones offered as a one-year trial that nudges hardware upgrades to Windows 11.

title: Google Cloud ups telecom game with Nokia AI agent infusion
blurb: Google Cloud and Nokia expanded their partnership with AI agents for autonomous network operations, built on Google's Agent Development Kit and Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform and deployed in Nokia's Assurance Center. Two of six agents are live now, with the rest arriving when the platform launches as a SaaS offering on Google Cloud Marketplace in September 2026, and Google claims the agents cut issue-resolution times by 50% to 80%.

title: Bold move, Cotton: Trump administration tells US techies it expects American quantum computer by 2028
blurb: The Trump administration set a 2028 target for US industry to deliver a working American-built quantum computer, framing the effort as a strategic race against China. The directive signals coming federal funding and procurement priorities around quantum hardware, putting IBM, Google, and IonQ in line for government contracts while raising questions about whether a fault-tolerant machine is achievable on that timeline.

title: IBM, Red Hat, Palo Alto team to secure open-source software
blurb: IBM, Red Hat, and Palo Alto Networks partnered to combine Palo Alto's Prisma network-based virtual patching with IBM and Red Hat's $5B Project Lightwell remediation effort, giving enterprises same-day protection against open-source vulnerabilities before official patches ship. The deal targets the 90% of Fortune 500 firms running OSS and counts Bank of America, JPMorgan Chase, Citi, Visa, and Mastercard among early adopters.

title: AI coding will soon get pricier than human developers
blurb: Gartner projects that AI coding tools will soon cost enterprises more per task than human developers, as token-based pricing scales with usage in ways fixed salaries do not. The warning for IT leaders is that without usage audits and defined objectives, AI spending overruns IT budgets rather than offsetting headcount. Gartner's prescription is unglamorous: track consumption, tie deployments to measurable outcomes, and treat AI coding as a metered line item, not a flat subscription.

title: TrueFoundry snaps up Seldon AI to unify enterprise ML and agentic AI under one roof
blurb: TrueFoundry acquired MLOps vendor Seldon AI to combine traditional ML and agentic AI under a single cloud-agnostic control plane, with financial terms undisclosed. The deal pairs TrueFoundry's AI Gateway, which routes prompts and governs API access at over one trillion tokens per day, with Seldon's Kubernetes-native model serving, positioning the combined platform as an independent alternative to the native AI tooling bundled with AWS, Google Cloud, and Azure.

title: Microsoft Access finally breaks free of its 22-inch form limit
blurb: Microsoft Access removed its decades-old 22-inch form-width limit, expanding the design canvas for legacy database apps.

title: Samsung and SK Hynix in discussions with South Korean government about future chip investments: report
blurb: Samsung and SK Hynix are in talks with South Korea's government over accelerating semiconductor fab investments, with officials signaling a new chip cluster announcement and a push to advance construction timelines by more than a decade to meet AI-driven demand. The pressure follows SK Hynix's $12.85B advanced-packaging plant and its rise to South Korea's most valuable company at a $1.35T market cap, ahead of new fabs from both firms due by 2027 and 2028.

title: Updates to Gemini in Google Classroom
blurb: Google added Gemini content-generation tools to Google Classroom, letting verified teachers create infographics, comic strips, and concept visualizations from the new Gemini tab, available now across Education Fundamentals, Standard, and Plus. Admins control access through age-based settings in the Admin console, with generation gated to users verified as teachers aged 18 or older, keeping the rollout within existing Workspace governance.

title: 파이브 아이즈 “AI 시대엔 사이버 위험도 경영 과제”…기업 리더 역할 확대 주문
blurb: The Five Eyes cybersecurity agencies, led by CISA and the UK's NCSC, urged CISOs and boards to treat AI-driven cyber risk as a core business risk, warning that AI will reshape both offensive and defensive capabilities in months rather than years. Critics including security advisor Joseph Steinberg called the guidance too generic, noting that four of its five recommended actions predate AI and never mention it.

title: Upscale AI readies Skyhammer scale-up networking tech, raises new funding
blurb: Upscale AI raised $190M in a Series A-1 extension led by Premji Invest, bringing total funding to $500M at a $2B valuation, with Nvidia, Salesforce Ventures, and Temasek joining the round. The startup is building Skyhammer, a purpose-built scale-up switch ASIC to link GPUs and XPUs at high speed, positioning itself as a pure-play backend-networking supplier to hyperscalers and neoclouds.

title: Connect to Google Meet hardware with room codes now in Early Preview
blurb: Google Meet added room codes in Early Preview, letting users connect to nearby conference hardware by entering a 5-character alphanumeric code on their personal device as a manual fallback when ultrasound proximity detection is unavailable or disabled. For IT admins, the feature cuts troubleshooting overhead in rooms with difficult acoustics or restricted proximity settings, with codes refreshing every few minutes and requiring a physical tap on the hardware to confirm presence.

title: Trump Seeks to Boost Quantum Computing With New Executive Orders
blurb: The Trump administration is preparing executive orders to accelerate US quantum computing development, including federal funding support and measures to help quantum firms commercialize their technology. The move signals stronger government backing for IBM, Google, and IonQ in the global race against China, and could open new procurement and grant channels for enterprises piloting post-quantum cryptography and early quantum workloads.

title: Windows 11 turns five, leaving some important lessons for Microsoft
blurb: Windows 11 marks its fifth anniversary, with The Register drawing lessons from Microsoft's rollout missteps and hardware-requirement controversies.

title: Filling out forms on mobile just got a lot easier thanks to Google Wallet
blurb: Google added Wallet integration to Chrome's Autofill on Android and iOS, letting the browser pull travel details, loyalty cards, vehicle information, and other credentials directly into web forms for tasks like flight check-ins and car rentals. The update extends Chrome's Enhanced Autofill system to mobile across both platforms and positions Google Wallet as a credential hub rather than a payments-only store.

title: Enhanced data resilience with cross-region backups in Backup and DR Service
blurb: Google Cloud made cross-region backups generally available in its Backup and DR Service, letting organizations send backups to a region entirely separate from the source workload, with initial support for Compute Engine, Disks, and Filestore, and Cloud SQL and AlloyDB to follow. The decoupling gives enterprises a cheaper alternative to multi-region backups while keeping control over data residency for requirements like GDPR.

title: Run isolated sandboxes with full lifecycle control: AWS Lambda introduces MicroVMs
blurb: AWS introduced Lambda MicroVMs, a Firecracker-backed serverless primitive that hands each user or session its own isolated, stateful execution environment with VM-level isolation, near-instant launch and resume, and pause-to-idle pricing. It targets multi-tenant apps that run untrusted user- or AI-generated code, such as coding assistants and analytics platforms, removing the choice between slow-booting VMs and containers that need custom kernel hardening.

title: Announcing Amazon EC2 G7 instances accelerated by NVIDIA RTX PRO 4500 Blackwell Server Edition GPUs
blurb: AWS made general availability for EC2 G7 instances, the first major cloud offering of NVIDIA RTX PRO 4500 Blackwell Server Edition GPUs, delivering up to 4.6x AI inference and 2.1x graphics performance over G6. Each instance scales to 8 GPUs and 256GB of GPU memory, with 700 Gbps of EFA networking (7x G6) and 7.6TB of local NVMe to keep large models and datasets close to compute.

title: How we found a bug in the hyper HTTP library
blurb: Cloudflare engineers spent six weeks tracing why their rearchitected Images binding intermittently returned truncated payloads, 200-status responses that cut a two-megabyte image down to a few hundred kilobytes, only for larger files. The culprit was a race condition in the hyper Rust HTTP library governing how processed image data streamed back to the client. The fix was four lines of code, a reminder that edge-network rollouts surface failure modes invisible under normal load.

title: Building Jaeger’s ClickHouse backend: 8.6× compression on 10 million spans
blurb: Jaeger v2.18.0 ships a native ClickHouse storage backend, giving teams a columnar alternative to Cassandra and Elasticsearch for distributed tracing. The maintainer measured 8.6× compression on 10 million spans, driven by the heavy repetition in trace data: service names, operation names, and status codes that a column-oriented OLAP store deduplicates cheaply. The post walks through the schema design and the trade-offs against the index-heavy operational overhead of the older backends.

title: AWS debuts Lambda MicroVMs with up to 8 hours runtime
blurb: AWS launched Lambda MicroVMs, extending the serverless runtime from a 15-minute cap to as long as 8 hours per invocation with hardware-isolated execution. The longer ceiling targets untrusted code, AI agents, and batch jobs that previously forced teams onto Fargate or EC2, though sustained 8-hour invocations shift the cost calculus closer to always-on compute than traditional event-driven Lambda.

title: Making secret scanning more trustworthy: Reducing false positives at scale
blurb: GitHub partnered with Microsoft Security & AI's Agents Offense team to add LLM-based contextual verification to its secret scanning, reasoning about how a detected value appears in code rather than whether it merely matches a secret-like pattern. The goal is to bring AI-detected secrets closer to the precision GitHub already achieves on provider-pattern detections across billions of pushes. Fewer low-value alerts means less triage toil and more developer trust in the system, without sacrificing coverage.

title: The post-quantum EO is an important milestone. Now it’s time to get to work
blurb: President Trump signed Executive Order 14409 on June 22, setting a December 31, 2030 deadline for federal agencies to move their most sensitive systems to post-quantum encryption and end-of-2031 for post-quantum authentication, with contractors required to meet post-quantum FIPS by 2030. Cloudflare, which already protects two-thirds of its browser traffic with post-quantum encryption, backs the order but wants OMB guidance to ease cost-effective agency migration.

title: GitHub Updates actions/checkout to Block Common Pwn Request Attack Patterns
blurb: GitHub updated actions/checkout to refuse fork pull request code in pull_request_target and workflow_run workflows by default, blocking the most common pwn request pattern that lets attackers steal the privileged GITHUB_TOKEN and secrets. The change ships in v7 effective June 18, 2026, backports to all supported major versions on July 16, and requires authors to set allow-unsafe-pr-checkout to true to opt out.

title: Decades-Old Squid Proxy Flaw ‘Squidbleed' Can Expose User Data
blurb: Researchers at Calif.io disclosed Squidbleed (CVE-2026-47729), a Heartbleed-style memory leak in Squid proxy's FTP parser that has existed since 1997 and can leak other users' cleartext HTTP request data, including credentials, session tokens, and API keys. The flaw is patched in Squid 7.6 and version 8, and poses the most risk in shared proxy environments like corporate networks and public Wi-Fi, where disabling unused FTP support is the recommended mitigation.

title: 'Cordyceps': Mushrooming Malicious Pull Requests Threaten Developer Workflows
blurb: Security researchers at Novee disclosed "Cordyceps," a class of CI/CD workflow weakness that lets attackers use malicious pull requests to steal the high-privilege signing keys and access tokens those automated pipelines hold, enabling command injection and supply chain compromise. A single scan flagged 654 repositories as potentially exploitable and confirmed 300 fully exploitable across Microsoft's Azure Sentinel, Google's Agent Development Kit, Apache Doris, Cloudflare's Workers SDK, and the PSF's Black.

title: Linux and Secure Boot certificate expiration (2025)
blurb: The Microsoft 2011 key used to sign the shim first-stage UEFI bootloader expires in September, after which Microsoft will stop signing the bootloader that Linux distributions rely on to boot under Secure Boot. A 2023 replacement key exists but is missing from many systems and often requires a vendor firmware update, so the breakage mainly hits new installs on Secure Boot hardware and adds work for distributors and users.

title: AWS unveils sovereign-centric micro serverless virtual machines
blurb: AWS released Lambda MicroVMs, a new Firecracker-backed resource that hands each user or session its own isolated environment with up to 16 vCPUs, 32 GB of memory, and 32 GB of disk for running untrusted or AI-generated code. Idle instances suspend via API or lifecycle policy while preserving memory and disk state, capping costs and targeting the data-sovereignty and isolation demands of multi-tenant AI workloads.

title: Google hits 50% IPv6
blurb: Google's measurements crossed 50% IPv6 adoption among its users for the first time, a milestone showing the protocol is mature and deployed at global scale across real-world networks. APNIC Labs pegs worldwide capability lower at 42% because it weights ad-based measurements with World Bank usage data, and its per-economy figures show adoption curves in India, Vietnam, and Saudi Arabia diverging sharply from the global trend.

title: Blinding the Watchmen: Abusing Cloud Logging Services for Defense Evasion and Visibility
blurb: Unit 42 researchers detail how attackers target cloud logging services like AWS CloudTrail and Google Cloud Logging to blind defenders, breaking down two attack classes: defense evasion that modifies or disables logging resources to execute unnoticed, and continuous visibility that siphons logs into attacker-controlled accounts. The writeup maps the specific configurations and detections platform teams need to catch logging-service tampering before it erases the audit trail you rely on for incident response.

title: Introducing the Cloudflare One stack: agent-powered deployment
blurb: Cloudflare released the Cloudflare One stack, a set of agent skills that configure, deploy, and manage a Zero Trust environment and map an existing security suite into Cloudflare One. The toolkit packages migration expertise from thousands of customer deployments, giving agents the network-topology and vendor-configuration context they otherwise lack to operate on SASE infrastructure.

title: Kops: Safely Extending the eBPF Compilation Pipeline with Native Operations
blurb: Kops is an extension interface that lets userspace compilers and kernel modules add new eBPF operations without touching the kernel core, keeping the trusted computing base minimal. Each operation ships as a verifier-checked proof sequence plus a native emit, the only per-operation addition to the TCB, with Lean 4 proofs confirming the two forms compute the same result. The authors' EInsn operations speed up eBPF microbenchmarks by up to 24% and production workloads by up to 12% on x86-64 and ARM64.

title: Verifiable, private AI: Google Cloud expands Confidential Computing frontiers
blurb: Google Cloud expanded its Confidential Computing lineup with Confidential G4 VMs and Confidential GKE Nodes, pairing NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell GPUs with 5th-gen AMD EPYC Turin CPUs to keep AI inference and fine-tuning data encrypted inside hardware TEEs, including in transit between CPU and GPU. The preview ships in every region the standard G4 is available, across On Demand, Reservations, DWS Flex Start, and Spot consumption models.

title: AWS Weekly Roundup: NY Summit recap, Local Zone in Hanoi, Grok 4.3 in Bedrock, price reductions, and more (June 22, 2026)
blurb: AWS recapped its NY Summit, where it introduced Continuum, an AI-native security service for the code vulnerability lifecycle, pushed Bedrock AgentCore to GA with a managed orchestration harness and the new Context service, and added release-gating to its DevOps Agent. The roundup also covers a new Local Zone in Hanoi with S3 and EBS local snapshots for data residency, Grok 4.3 landing in Bedrock, and several price reductions.

title: Log Analytics is now Observability Analytics: Query logs and traces with SQL
blurb: Google Cloud renamed Log Analytics to Observability Analytics and made trace data and the configuration Observability API generally available, letting teams run SQL across logs and distributed trace spans in one place without moving or duplicating data. SREs can now join application logs to trace spans in a single query, surfacing slow checkout requests or P95 latency on external AI agent tool calls for faster root-cause analysis.

title: Upbound open-sources Modelplane to optimize inference clusters
blurb: Upbound open-sourced Modelplane, a Crossplane-derived control plane that manages AI inference clusters across multiple clouds, automatically placing workloads and spinning up replicas as request volume grows. It cuts cold-start latency with distributed caching of model weights on local storage and routes prompts through a gateway that enforces cost and security policy and fails over to an external environment during outages.

title: Microsoft’s next big thing for the cloud: an agent that keeps its cool when everything falls apart
blurb: Microsoft made its Azure Copilot Observability Agent generally available, an LLM agent that correlates logs, metrics, and traces across a company's systems to surface the likely root cause of an incident, plus an autonomous-operations preview that triages alerts unprompted. It stops short of acting, leaving restarts and config changes to humans, and ships usage-based pricing into a crowded field already holding Datadog's Bits AI SRE and AWS's DevOps Agent.

title: A Comparison of Kubernetes Compliance Standards and Configuration Scanners
blurb: This paper benchmarks eight Kubernetes hardening guidelines into 79 configuration recommendations, then runs ten popular static config scanners against it. The authors find substantial gaps in which issues each guideline and scanner actually covers, plus inconsistent scoring and ranking of the same misconfigurations across tools. The practical takeaway for platform teams is that no single scanner is authoritative, and severity rankings from tools like kube-bench or Checkov should be treated as one opinion rather than ground truth.

title: OpenAI Codex bombards SSDs with needless write operations, costing millions
blurb: Researchers found that OpenAI's Codex agent issues a flood of redundant SSD write operations through a clumsy logging implementation, persisting data with no regard for cost and racking up millions in unnecessary storage wear and spend. The waste lands on infrastructure teams running Codex at scale, where amplified write amplification shortens SSD lifespan and inflates cloud storage bills that nobody budgeted for.

title: Agent Auth: A lawyer’s day in court
blurb: The author uses a courtroom analogy to break down authentication for AI agents, mapping a lawyer representing a client to the layers an agent platform must implement: agent identity, principal identity, On-Behalf-Of delegation tokens, and policy enforcement. The argument treats agents as microservices-plus, demanding stricter auth because one agent acts for many users, plus guardrails and observability over prompts, tool calls, and request contents.

title: Explainer: Why your legacy storage is choking your expensive GPU
blurb: The author argues that legacy storage arrays, built for spinning-disk throughput, can't feed data fast enough to keep GPU clusters busy, leaving expensive accelerators idle while they wait on I/O. The explainer walks through why traditional NAS and SAN designs bottleneck training and inference pipelines, and makes the case for parallel file systems and NVMe-backed storage tiers as the fix. The cost angle is the point: an idle GPU is wasted spend, so storage that can saturate it pays for itself.

title: One vulnerability view: From scanner coverage to AI governance
blurb: GitLab 19.1 adds a single vulnerability view that enforces any SARIF-emitting third-party scanner across every project under one policy, eliminating the per-project drift that lets new repos ship unscanned for weeks. Third-party findings now flow through GitLab Duo's auto-remediation, with SAST false-positive triage and agentic resolution opening ready-to-merge fixes, plus a beta of AI audit event streaming to track agent behavior.

title: Spotlight on WG Device Management
blurb: Kubernetes graduated Dynamic Resource Allocation to GA, the cornerstone deliverable of the Device Management Working Group's effort to schedule GPUs, TPUs, and network interfaces beyond the legacy CPU-and-memory model. The new structured-parameters API supports allocating accelerators after pod start and time-sharing hardware, targeting the AI, edge, and telecom workloads that the original device-manager design could not handle at scale.

title: Advancing AI agent security in Vault
blurb: HashiCorp moved native AI-agent support in Vault Enterprise to public preview, making the OAuth 2.0 `authorization_details` claim (RFC 9396 Rich Authorization Requests) required by default so access is evaluated per request rather than through broad, long-lived identity permissions. The secure-by-default baseline rejects requests lacking fine-grained scope unless configured otherwise, giving platform teams least-privilege enforcement and auditable, per-action authorization for agents at scale.

title: GitLab Patch Release: 19.1.1, 19.0.3, 18.11.6
blurb: GitLab shipped patch releases 19.1.1, 19.0.3, and 18.11.6 fixing a CVSS 8.7 stored XSS; self-managed installs should upgrade now.

title: Lighthouse agentic browsing scoring
blurb: Chrome's Lighthouse added an Agentic Browsing category that runs deterministic audits on how well a site supports machine interaction, checking WebMCP tool registration and accessibility-tree integrity and reporting a fractional pass ratio rather than the usual 0-100 score. Marketers tracking AI-search and agent traffic now have a CI-friendly signal for whether their pages are structured for agents to read and act on, alongside the SEO checks they already run.

title: MoEngage buys Aampe to bet enterprise marketing’s future belongs to per-customer AI agents, not segments
blurb: MoEngage acquired San Francisco-based Aampe in an all-cash deal worth tens of millions, betting that enterprise marketing shifts from segment-based campaigns to a dedicated autonomous AI agent shadowing each individual customer. The acquisition strengthens MoEngage's "agentic" pitch as it wins multi-million-dollar ACV migrations from Salesforce Marketing Cloud and Adobe Experience Cloud, with Aampe's per-user personalization already running at brands like Swiggy and Grab.

title: Adobe Just Made its Biggest AI Push Yet, and it Stretches from Photoshop to Disney World
blurb: Adobe rolled out five AI announcements in three days, led by a Firefly AI Assistant now in public beta inside Photoshop, Premiere, Illustrator, InDesign, and Frame.io, plus a Firefly Foundry deal training custom models on Disney Imagineering's own catalogue. The strategy is to become the creative and marketing AI layer spanning solo Photoshop users to enterprise brands, with IP-clean models pitched as the wedge against scraped-data rivals like Canva and Figma.

title: India’s MoEngage bets that the future of marketing is millions of AI agents
blurb: MoEngage acquired San Francisco-based Aampe in an all-cash deal reportedly worth tens of millions, betting that assigning a dedicated AI agent to each customer will replace traditional audience segments and campaign rules. The deal sharpens MoEngage's pitch to enterprises migrating off Salesforce Marketing Cloud and Adobe Experience Cloud, where it has already closed several multimillion-dollar switches and now serves 1,350 brands across 75 countries.

title: Publicité : WALMART rachète VIBE pour 1,2 milliard d’euros afin d’accélérer sa riposte face à Amazon
blurb: Walmart is acquiring French adtech firm Vibe.co for roughly $1.4B to expand Walmart Connect into connected TV and close the retail-media gap with Amazon Ads, which already generates tens of billions in annual revenue. The deal follows Walmart's $2.3B Vizio purchase and signals a push to turn its transactional shopper data into CTV ad inventory as streaming nears 45% of US TV viewing.

title: Cannes Lions 2026: Strengthen creative campaigns with new tools from YouTube
blurb: At Cannes Lions 2026, Google announced new Gemini-built insights tools for YouTube, including more granular U.S. trend data and integrated brand pulse metrics inside Google Ads Insights Finder. A new Content & Creator Insights API gives agencies richer creator and audience data for media planning, while Gemini will soon surface creative tips to optimize Demand Gen campaigns.

title: Why the National Basketball Players Association is debuting a B2C brand
blurb: The National Basketball Players Association launched Plyrs Untd, its first consumer-facing commercial brand, giving members more control over how their names, images, and likenesses are used in merch, content, and partnerships. The brand debuted at Cannes Lions with "Own the Game," a campaign film starring 26 NBA players, signaling a shift for marketers from advertising around player culture to co-creating with the athletes directly.

title: GEO is following the same path as early SEO
blurb: Generative engine optimization is repeating early SEO's arc, where today's winning tactics either harden into best practice or get penalized as engines mature. The author argues good SEO informs but won't maximize AI-answer visibility, and the current low-competition window favors teams optimizing now with question-based headlines, TL;DR summaries, FAQs, and schema. Black-hat shortcuts like AI-generated spam and fake E-E-A-T signals may post early gains, but SEO's history suggests durable advantage comes from proprietary, genuinely useful content.

title: WSJ: $1.9M in Fake Bets Propped Up Polymarket Creator Videos
blurb: A Wall Street Journal investigation found that roughly $1.9M in bets shown across 1,105 Polymarket-promoting creator videos were fake, staged on dummy versions of the platform and produced by paid clippers who were told not to disclose the arrangement. The fabricated-engagement campaign targeted U.S. audiences as Polymarket pursues regulatory approval and institutional backing, a reminder that influencer-driven proof can be manufactured wholesale.

title: Advertisers may finally see who really touches their bid requests
blurb: IAB Tech Lab proposed SupplyChain v1.1, an update to OpenRTB's schain object that would expose every company that processes, routes, or enriches a programmatic bid request, not just those in the payment flow. The change reframes supply-path optimization away from simply minimizing intermediaries, letting buyers evaluate what each participant contributes; public comment runs through Aug. 21, 2026.

title: Average Organic Traffic Benchmarks From Real Websites (June 2026)
blurb: Ahrefs published median monthly organic traffic benchmarks built from anonymized Google Search Console data across 422,421 real websites, broken out by industry, Domain Rating, and site size. News leads at 175,115 median monthly clicks while Law & Government sits at the bottom at 8,607, and the median runs several times below the mean in nearly every category because a few giant publishers skew the average. Use the cohort closest to your own DR and size as a growth target rather than any single industry-wide number.

title: Creator Content Now Accounts for 44% of Paid Media Creative, Exposing a New Gap Between Leaders and Laggards
blurb: Creator content now accounts for 44% of paid media creative on average, according to CreatorIQ research that surveyed 100 paid media managers and found 92% of leaders and executives now run creator assets in paid campaigns, up from roughly half that two years ago. CreatorIQ reports a large ROI gap between leaders and laggards, with the differentiator being whether brands have built the measurement, rights, and paid media infrastructure to deploy creator content at scale.

title: What is a Good Organic CTR? Real Website Benchmarks (June 2026)
blurb: Whole-site organic CTR now sits between 1% and 2% for most sites, based on aggregated Google Search Console data from 422,421 websites, with the median running well below the position-1 figures most teams cite. CTR is falling fastest in answer-heavy niches: Pets & Animals (0.80%) and Health (1.12%) trigger AI Overviews on 37% to 43% of queries. The takeaway for content teams is to shift effort away from informational keywords AI now answers and toward intent and formats Overviews rarely intercept.

title: AppsFlyer Reportedly Lands $1B At $2.7B Valuation To Help Companies Track Digital Ads
blurb: AppsFlyer raised more than $1B in a Series E at a $2.7B post-money valuation, with Moloco, Google, Meta, and Unity each taking minority stakes in the mobile attribution platform. The investor list signals that as AI automates ad buying, marketers increasingly want independent measurement of return on ad spend rather than attribution numbers self-reported by the same platforms selling the inventory.

title: Meta launches advanced AI ad features
blurb: Meta is rolling out new AI ad tools across its platforms, including generative creative solutions, automated text generation, and built-in A/B testing mechanisms for campaigns. The additions push more of the creative and optimization workload onto Meta's automation, narrowing the gap between advertisers running lean teams and those with full in-house production.

title: Heinz blacks out its logo in World Cup stunt
blurb: Heinz Canada turned the World Cup's strict sponsorship rules to its advantage, launching an "Unofficial Stadium Ketchup" with a blacked-out label after its branding was taped over at venues like Toronto's BMO Field. The stunt, alongside similar moves from Levi's and Gillette, is generating more organic social buzz than many paid FIFA sponsors, a reminder that owning a cultural moment can outperform the sponsorship slot itself.

title: Meta launches new AI glasses models
blurb: Meta launched three new AI glasses models, including a collaboration with Kylie Jenner, at a lower price point than its previous releases. The cheaper lineup widens the addressable market for wearable AI and gives marketers a larger installed base to plan camera-first, voice, and AR ad formats against.

title: Stop trying to replace people with AI
blurb: The author argues that "substitution positioning," selling AI as a replacement for people, is the cardinal marketing mistake of the AI era: it wins attention now but erodes trust with the buyers and employees you need later. The evidence is that the replacement narrative keeps failing in public, with software-engineering demand hitting new highs after Anthropic's Dario Amodei predicted SWE jobs would vanish, and customer-service hiring outpacing the broader market after Sam Altman's similar call. The fix is to position AI as augmentation that expands what teams can do, not as a headcount cut.

title: 9 new LinkedIn features + what to do with each one
blurb: LinkedIn shipped nine new features this month, including plain-English search, a follower-versus-out-of-network reach split, paid 1:1 Advice Sessions, a Creator Marketplace for brand deals, $10/day mobile Boost, and an algorithm change that penalizes generic AI content. The reach split lets creators finally separate audience-building from raw impressions, and the AI-slop penalty rewards distinct, original viewpoints over interchangeable text posts.

title: Better search, worse web
blurb: Google reports that AI Overviews now reach over a billion people and have driven a double-digit rise in search usage, but the author argues that every gain in answer quality removes a reason for users to click through to the sites that supplied the answers. DuckDuckGo installs jumped roughly 30% after Google's 2026 I/O, a signal that some users want a search they can switch off.

title: Beehiiv adds Cloudflare AI Crawl Control so writers can block or allow bots
blurb: Beehiiv added a Cloudflare AI Crawl Control toggle, letting its 135,000 publishers block or allow AI crawlers from the dashboard.

title: Walmart s’offre Vibe, adtech lancée par deux Français
blurb: Walmart agreed to acquire Vibe, the French-founded streaming-TV ad platform, folding its team into Walmart Connect to expand the retailer's retail-media reach into connected TV. The deal lands six months after Vibe's $50M Series B and bets on CTV's momentum, with streaming now 45% of US TV viewing and digital video ad spend growing roughly 3x faster than the overall ad market.

title: Facebook rolls out an AI companion app for creators
blurb: Facebook is reimagining Creator Studio as a standalone AI companion app, now testing with select creators, that surfaces personalized posting recommendations, drafts comment replies in the creator's tone, and answers performance questions like "when should I post?" The move is Meta's bid to keep creators inside Facebook rather than TikTok or YouTube, and to displace third-party tools like ChatGPT from their content and analytics workflow.

title: We used context engineering to 5x conversion and 2x activation
blurb: PostHog rebuilt its onboarding into an AI wizard that integrates the product into any codebase from a single CLI command, cutting two hours of setup to eight minutes and driving a 5x conversion and 2x activation lift. The team's core finding: for high-variance onboarding, reasoning wasn't the bottleneck, context supply was, and marginal agent-layer tuning lost to simply feeding the agent far more product, SDK, and framework context. They restructured their docs team into context engineers maintaining a company-wide context layer the agent pulls from, and open-sourced the wizard to copy.

title: Meta launches integrated booking for lead ads
blurb: Meta launched integrated booking for lead ads, letting prospects schedule directly through connected third-party tools like Calendly without leaving the form. The feature is Facebook-only at launch and aims to cut signup friction, which should lift lead-to-meeting conversion for demand-gen teams running appointment-based funnels on the platform.

title: Understanding AI Visibility: How to Win Brand Influence in LLMs
blurb: Buyers now start sales-tool research inside ChatGPT, Perplexity, Google AI Mode, and Claude, where the model assembles a ranked vendor shortlist during a hidden selection phase before any measurable touchpoint. Foundation's report on 50 B2B brands found only 10.15% of AI citations point to brand-owned domains, falling to 2.2% on unbranded category questions that actually build shortlists. The takeaway for marketers is to treat generative engine optimization as a distinct channel, earning citations across the third-party sources LLMs trust rather than optimizing only owned pages.

title: Americans and AI 2026: Chatbots, Smart Devices, and Views on Impact
blurb: Pew's 2026 survey of 5,119 US adults found that about half now use AI chatbots, up sharply from summer 2024, with roughly one in four using them daily and search and work as the top use cases. Sentiment runs negative, including among younger adults, as more respondents expect AI to harm rather than help society and majorities say it is advancing too fast and threatens their personal data.

title: How product management can fix your AI integration problems
blurb: The author argues that the widely cited MIT stat about enterprise AI pilots stalling at proof-of-concept isn't an indictment of the technology, but of dropping new tools onto processes designed for a slower era. He pairs it with a second figure: roughly 84% of organizations haven't redesigned a single workflow around the AI they bought. The fix is product management's core discipline, prioritizing outcomes over outputs and reshaping how work moves through the org rather than treating the purchase as the transformation.

title: Figma adds code layers, support for animations, more AI features in new update
blurb: Figma's latest update adds code layers to its collaborative canvas, native support for animations, transitions, and 3D transforms, and AI-generated shader effects, alongside custom skills and plug-ins users can create from prompts. The code layers let teams clone repos and pull flows into design for low-stakes iteration, signaling Figma's push to own more of the design-to-engineering handoff rather than cede it to dedicated prototyping tools.

title: We used context engineering to 5x conversion and 2x activation
blurb: PostHog documented how it built an open-source onboarding wizard that integrates the product into any codebase via one CLI command, cutting two hours of setup to eight minutes and lifting conversion 5x and activation 2x. The team's main lesson: for high-variance tasks, reasoning isn't the bottleneck, context supply is, so agent-layer tricks like multi-step planning and compaction yielded marginal gains while feeding the agent a company-wide context layer drove the results. They reframed their docs team as context engineers maintaining that library.

title: Base Beryl: B20 Token Standard, Faster Withdrawals, Reth V2
blurb: Base launched its Beryl testnet ahead of a June 25 mainnet upgrade, adding the B20 token standard for native issuance, faster L2-to-L1 withdrawals, and a Reth V2 execution client. The Reth V2 upgrade targets higher throughput and capital efficiency, positioning Base as an issuance platform rather than only a general-purpose Optimism-stack rollup.

title: Jaredfromsubway.eth, Ethereum's Most Active Sandwich Bot, Drained for $7.5M Over the Weekend
blurb: An attacker drained more than $7.5M from jaredfromsubway.eth, the Ethereum address responsible for roughly 70% of all sandwich attacks on the network between late 2024 and October 2025, in what Blockaid identified as a counter-MEV honeypot rather than a phishing or smart-contract exploit. It is the largest single-event loss publicly tied to an MEV operator to date, with some of the stolen WETH, USDC, and USDT already routed through Tornado Cash.

title: Warwick Takes Personal Blame for sUSD Mismanagement, Charts Basis-Vault Replacement
blurb: Synthetix founder Kain Warwick took personal responsibility for sUSD, which has traded below peg for over a year, and laid out a plan to wind down the SNX-backed stablecoin and replace it with a basis-vault instrument running on the protocol's v4 exchange. Governance's SIP-423 would freeze the sUSD contract and pay holders four SNX per token, with the treasury already holding roughly 30% of outstanding supply.

title: Ethereum Foundation Cuts Budget 40% in Sweeping Restructuring
blurb: The Ethereum Foundation is cutting its annual budget by roughly 40% and laying off about 20% of staff, 54 employees, as it restructures into an endowment-based nonprofit organized around five divisions. The shift lowers the EF's target spending rate from roughly 15% of remaining funds annually to about 5% after 2030, aiming to fund protocol work through extended downturns without relying on token sales.

title: Secret Network's Axelar Bridge Drained $4.67M via Infinite-Mint Flaw
blurb: Secret Network suspended its Axelar bridge after an attacker exploited a minting flaw to drain $4.67M in wrapped tokens over seven undetected days, forging IBC packets through a self-run validator chain that minted unbacked assets against missing escrow checks. The contract bug dated to March 2023 and survived a March 2026 migration, with Secret's default transaction encryption hiding the shortfall until a failed transfer surfaced it.

title: Aztec Reaches L2Beat Stage 2 After Governance Revokes Rollup Contract Ownership
blurb: Aztec, the privacy-focused Ethereum layer-2, reached Stage 2 on L2Beat after an on-chain governance action revoked ownership of its rollup contract, making the code immutable with no admin override and a permanent user exit window. The designation is L2Beat's top decentralization tier, removing the security-council backstop that most rollups retain and leaving Aztec governed entirely by smart contracts.

title: Microsoft discovers new lightweight backdoor that steals cryptocurrency
blurb: Microsoft detected a self-propagating worm it calls Crypto Clipper that spreads via USB .lnk files and scans clipboards for wallet addresses and seed phrases, exfiltrating credentials and screenshots to attacker servers over a portable Tor client routed through a local SOCKS5 proxy. The malware blends data theft with remote code execution, functioning as a lightweight backdoor without traditional installers or exposed IP-based command-and-control infrastructure.

title: Ethereum Foundation’s Interim ED Lays Out Execution Plan: MEV Elimination, Default Privacy, ETH Pay
blurb: The Ethereum Foundation's interim Executive Director, Bastian Aue, published a six-part execution thread committing the EF to treating toxic MEV extraction as core protocol work, making privacy a network default, and shifting EF compensation into ETH and Ethereum-native stablecoins. The thread frames MEV as the next front in a longer cypherpunk effort and targets privileged orderflow, cartelized builders, and trusted relays as structural failure modes to eliminate.

title: Circle Publishes Official USDC Spec for Machine Payments Protocol, Enabling Crosschain Agent-to-Agent Commerce
blurb: Circle published a formal USDC method specification for the Machine Payments Protocol, standardizing how AI agents settle USDC payments across EVM chains and Solana via an HTTP 402 challenge-response flow and EIP-3009 authorizations. The spec adds USDC as a named MPP method type, introduces the protocol's first crosschain payment profile through Circle Gateway, and extends coverage to USDCx on Stacks for Bitcoin-layer settlement.

title: 0x Opens Swap API to AI Agents Paying $0.01 Per Request in USDC
blurb: 0x opened its Swap API to autonomous AI agents, who can now pay $0.01 per request in USDC from their own wallets with no API key or account setup, using the HTTP 402 standard via Alchemy AgentPay. Payment is accepted through x402 on Base and Solana or the Machine Payment Protocol, extending 0x's cross-DEX liquidity aggregation to agents that need onchain pricing or routing.

title: Senate Passes Housing Bill With Fed CBDC Ban Through 2030 in 85-5 Vote
blurb: The US Senate passed the 21st Century ROAD to Housing Act in an 85-5 bipartisan vote, attaching a statutory ban on a Federal Reserve central bank digital currency through December 31, 2030 to the broader housing package. The provision carves out permissionless, cash-like dollar stablecoins, leaving private-sector issuers untouched while the bill heads to a House floor vote and then to President Trump.

title: Bank of England Publishes Policy Statement and Draft Rules for Systemic Stablecoins
blurb: The Bank of England published a policy statement and draft Code of Practice for systemic sterling stablecoins, replacing proposed per-user holding caps with a £40B issuance ceiling per coin and a backing-asset mix of up to 70% short-dated UK gilts and at least 30% unremunerated central bank deposits. Issuers can file feedback until September 22, with HM Treasury, not the BoE, designating which coins count as systemic.

title: NYSE Parent ICE and OKX Form 50-50 Joint Venture for Tokenized Equities, Co-Chaired by Andrew Cuomo
blurb: Intercontinental Exchange, the parent of the NYSE, and crypto exchange OKX are forming a 50-50 joint venture to bring NYSE-listed equities on-chain, with former New York Governor Andrew Cuomo co-chairing. Pending regulatory approval, the venture will operate as a US-registered broker-dealer and futures commission merchant, building on ICE's March investment that valued OKX at $25B.

title: THORChain Resumes Trading After Month-Long Halt From $10.7M Exploit
blurb: THORChain restored full operations Tuesday, bringing signing, churning, swaps, and liquidity-provider actions back online after a six-week halt tied to a $10.7M May exploit that drained Bitcoin, Ethereum, BNB Chain, and Base vaults. The restart followed vault and keyshare verification, though it leaves unresolved questions from security firm V12, which alleges THORChain silently patched a near-identical bug, skipped the bounty, then retired the program entirely.

title: Zelle to Launch Stablecoin and Expand to India
blurb: Early Warning, the bank consortium behind Zelle and owned by JPMorgan, Bank of America, Wells Fargo, and four others, will launch a dollar-backed stablecoin called ZelleUSD (ZLUSD) later this year while expanding the app to India. The move puts the largest US P2P transfer network, bigger than Venmo or Cash App, directly into stablecoin rails aimed at cross-border remittance corridors.

title: Congress Passes Fed CBDC Ban Through 2030, Sends Bill to Trump
blurb: Congress passed the 21st Century ROAD to Housing Act, which bars the Federal Reserve from issuing a central bank digital currency through December 31, 2030, sending the first statutory CBDC prohibition in US history to Trump for signature. Paired with the GENIUS Act's stablecoin framework, the ban cements Washington's digital-dollar posture around private dollar tokens rather than a Fed-issued retail dollar.

title: Zuckerberg Orders Meta to Build Standalone Prediction Market App, Codenamed Arena
blurb: Meta is building a standalone prediction-market app codenamed Arena, per New York Times reporting, launching with a video game-style points system rather than real-money wagering, though the company hasn't ruled out cash betting later. The move pits Meta's 3.56 billion daily active users against crypto-native venues like Polymarket and Kalshi, and sent Robinhood and DraftKings shares lower in early trading this week.

title: Aztec Tells V4 Users to Withdraw Before June 25 as V5 Upgrade Exposes Security Flaws
blurb: Aztec urged users to withdraw funds from its Alpha V4 layer-2 before June 25, when a governance vote advancing the V5 upgrade will publicly disclose a critical V4 vulnerability the team has kept private since March 2026. The bug affects the proving system and could enable theft of user funds, leaving any assets left on V4 portals exposed in a version with known, unpatched flaws.

title: Morgan Stanley Files Amended S-1s for Spot Solana and Ethereum Trusts
blurb: Morgan Stanley filed amended S-1s for spot Solana and Ethereum trusts, naming BNY Mellon and Coinbase Custody as joint custodians, a 0.14% sponsor fee, and NYSE Arca tickers MSOL and MSSE. The fee matches its Bitcoin ETF MSBT and undercuts rival altcoin issuers, though no effective date is set and demand for single-asset altcoin wrappers remains unproven.

title: Crypto Heist Fueled by Elaborate Fake Reputation-Boosting Campaign
blurb: Check Point uncovered a clipboard-hijacking campaign that fakes credibility across GitHub, SourceForge, and YouTube to drain Windows and macOS crypto wallets.

title: Why Zelle wants to launch a stablecoin
blurb: Zelle announced Zelle USD, a stablecoin for international payments, just a week after the bank consortium TCH committed to tokenized deposits instead. The split reflects different jobs: stablecoins move dollars across any wallet 24/7 on public chains, while tokenized deposits keep one bank's yield and protections but settle only inside a closed network.

title: SecondFi loses $2.4 million in Cardano wallet exploit
blurb: SecondFi, the Cardano wallet formerly known as Yoroi, confirmed three attacks drained 16 million ADA ($2.4 million) from 374 wallets via a flaw in its proprietary wallet-generation software, and rescued a further 129 million ADA to a third-party custodian. Security firm SlowMist estimates total losses could exceed $20 million pending an independent audit, with affected users unable to self-protect by moving their seed phrase.

title: Digital euro clears key hurdle as EU seeks to break free from U.S. credit cards
blurb: The European Parliament's economic committee approved draft rules for the digital euro, a central-bank-backed electronic wallet, advancing a six-year ECB project aimed at cutting the euro zone's reliance on Visa and Mastercard. The ECB plans a 12-month pilot starting in the second half of 2027 ahead of a full launch in 2029, with final EU-level approval targeted by year-end.

title: Taiko Bridge Drained $1.7M After SGX Signing Key Left Exposed on GitHub
blurb: Taiko's L2 bridge lost roughly $1.7M after an attacker used an RSA-3072 signing key left exposed in the public taikoxyz/raiko GitHub repo to enroll a rogue SGX prover and forge withdrawal proofs accepted by its Ethereum L1 contracts. The Security Council paused the bridge and halted block production, while Upbit, Bithumb, and KuCoin suspended TAIKO transfers after the attacker moved ~1.99M tokens to MEXC.

title: Who gets a direct line to the Fed? Congress weighs risks of Fed ‘skinny accounts’ for crypto and fintech firms
blurb: The House Financial Services Committee debated the Federal Reserve's proposed "skinny" master accounts, which would give crypto-focused banks and fintechs direct access to the central bank's payment rails without routing through partner banks. The crypto industry called the proposal "overdue" while community banks warned that novel firms aren't bound by the same compliance regime, and the Kansas City Fed has already approved a limited-purpose account for Kraken parent Payward.

title: SBI Group Plans to Issue Yen Stablecoin JPYSC as Early as This Week, Nikkei Reports
blurb: SBI Group, the Japanese financial conglomerate with $252B in assets, plans to issue its yen-linked stablecoin JPYSC as early as this week after receiving FSA approval, making it the first Japanese institution to issue a trust-bank-structured stablecoin under the Payment Services Act. The trust-bank classification carries no remittance cap, unlike JPYC's fund-transfer license, opening JPYSC to enterprise treasury settlement and large-volume institutional transactions.

title: Trump lands in Senate's crosshairs over $500 million UAE investment in his crypto venture
blurb: Senate Democrats requested hearings into a $500M investment by UAE officials in World Liberty Financial, the Trump family crypto venture, citing a 49% stake and $218M in upfront payments to entities tied to the Trump family and Middle East diplomat Steve Witkoff. The letter, signed by Warren, Blumenthal, Peters, Durbin, and Wyden, seeks sworn testimony on whether the deal shaped subsequent US policy toward the Gulf state.

title: Ethereum Foundation Cuts 20% of Staff in Sweeping Reorganization
blurb: The Ethereum Foundation cut 54 employees, roughly 20% of its staff, concluding a months-long reorganization tied to its updated Treasury Management Policy and a plan to lower annual operating expenses from about 15% of treasury to a 5% baseline by 2030. The cuts follow nine senior departures since January and come as the EF's ETH holdings sit near a six-year low of about $209 million by dollar value.

title: Ethlabs Will Overlap with the Ethereum Foundation and Draw Its 'Densest Talent,' Funders Say
blurb: Ethlabs, a new Ethereum research lab backed by the network's two largest corporate holders, launched this week to complement the Ethereum Foundation, though its own funders concede it will compete for the same talent and EIP slots. The launch marks Ethereum's clearest bet yet on a fragmented, multi-organization development model, a deliberate contrast with Solana's single-foundation approach across a network worth roughly $194 billion.

title: Binance says it will make a fresh push for permission to operate in the EU after its MiCA license application in Greece failed ahead of the June 30 deadline (Reuters)
blurb: Binance will reapply for permission to operate across the EU after its bid for a MiCA license in Greece collapsed ahead of the June 30 deadline for the bloc's crypto framework. The setback leaves the exchange without a passportable EU license while rivals like Coinbase and OKX have already secured MiCA approval, narrowing Binance's regulated access to the European market.

title: World Liberty Financial's USD1 Supply Grows 9.7% in a Week to $4.85 Billion
blurb: World Liberty Financial's USD1 stablecoin grew its circulating supply 9.7% in seven days to $4.85 billion, roughly $427 million in new tokens, making it the fourth-largest dollar-pegged stablecoin behind Tether, USDC, and Sky's USDS. The mint coincides with Aster routing real-world-asset perpetual settlement exclusively through USD1, even as competitors Sky's USDS and PayPal's PYUSD contracted over the same window.

title: DoJ Seizes Huione Cloud Account Tied to Cyber Scam Money Laundering
blurb: The DoJ seized a cloud computing account hosting backend infrastructure for subsidiaries of Cambodia-based HuiOne Group, while Treasury sanctioned nine individuals and 26 entities tied to the affiliated Prince Group. HuiOne's Telegram-based marketplace moved billions in cryptocurrency between 2021 and 2025, offering escrow, money laundering, and crimeware services that funneled Southeast Asian scam-center proceeds into the banking system.

title: Cboe launches prediction market suite with binary option contracts on S&P 500
blurb: Cboe launched a prediction market suite featuring binary option contracts tied to the S&P 500, putting the regulated exchange directly into a market segment dominated by crypto-native platforms like Polymarket and Kalshi. The contracts are live on Interactive Brokers and are expected to reach Charles Schwab in the coming months, extending event-contract trading to mainstream retail brokerage users.

title: Uniswap Adds No-Code Token Auction Tool to Its Web App
blurb: Uniswap added a no-code token auction tool to its web app, letting any team configure and launch an onchain sale through a four-step browser flow built on its Continuous Clearing Auction mechanism. CCA accumulates bids across multiple blocks at a single carried-forward clearing price, removing the speed edge that favors bots, and routes closing liquidity directly into a Uniswap pool.

title: Trump White House Negotiating CLARITY Act Ethics Deal With Senate Democrats
blurb: A Trump White House official is now directly negotiating an ethics compromise with Senate Democrats on the CLARITY Act, the last sticking point before the crypto market structure bill can reach a Senate floor vote. The dispute centers on language restricting senior officials' crypto holdings, complicated by the estimated $2.3B Trump and his family have generated from crypto ventures since he returned to office.

title: Securitize Wraps Roubini's SEC-Registered ETF as Dubai VARA Digital Security
blurb: Securitize will tokenize economist Nouriel Roubini's SEC-registered Atlas America Fund into USAFi, a digital security issued under Dubai's VARA Asset Referenced Virtual Asset Rulebook and custodied at BNY, targeting a Q3 2026 launch. The structure lets the Nasdaq-listed ETF trade permissionlessly with 24/7 settlement, giving non-US institutions regulated, portable collateral on DeFi rails and adding to Securitize's franchise alongside BlackRock's BUIDL.

title: Ripple Wins Preliminary MiCA CASP Approval in Luxembourg, Unlocking EEA Passporting
blurb: Ripple received a preliminary CASP license from Luxembourg's CSSF under MiCA, a conditional approval that clears it to passport its payments platform across all 30 EEA states once final conditions are met. Combined with its existing EU Electronic Money Institution license, the authorization will let European banks and fintechs access Ripple's collect-exchange-pay-out stack through a single integration.

title: CFTC Opens Comment on 24/7 Energy Futures and Perpetual Oil Contracts
blurb: The CFTC issued a request for comment on running standard futures on a 24/7 schedule and listing perpetual contracts tied to physically delivered energy commodities like crude oil. The move signals interest in importing crypto-native perpetual designs into oil and gas derivatives, with comments due within 30 days of Federal Register publication ahead of any formal rulemaking.

title: Franklin Templeton Closes 250 Digital Acquisition, Launches Franklin Crypto for Institutional Allocators
blurb: Franklin Templeton closed its acquisition of 250 Digital and launched Franklin Crypto, an actively managed digital-asset unit targeting pensions, sovereign wealth funds, and other institutional allocators, with part of the consideration paid in its on-chain BENJI tokens. The deal folds CoinFund's former liquid crypto strategies into the $1.78T manager and adds a third institutional product line alongside its BENJI tokenized-treasury fund and pending Bitcoin DRIP ETFs.

title: Coinbase's Tokenized Stocks With 'True Equity Ownership'
blurb: Coinbase announced tokenized stocks marketed as offering "true equity ownership," but disclosed no details on the underlying legal structure that determines whether holders own actual shares or a derivative claim. That structure governs the regulatory treatment, redemption rights, and competitive positioning against rivals like Robinhood and Kraken, making it the decisive open question behind the launch.

title: Sonic Labs Overhauls Board and Leadership as Cronje Steps Down
blurb: Sonic Labs announced a board and leadership overhaul as co-founder Andre Cronje stepped down from his executive role, alongside a candid update on the protocol's current standing and forward commitments. The shakeup removes one of DeFi's most prominent founder-leaders from day-to-day operations and signals a governance reset for the chain formerly known as Fantom.

title: Black Lake, Nuva Labs tokenize $25 million in mortgage loans on Provenance in RWA push
blurb: Black Lake Digital Markets and Nuva Labs completed what they call the first onchain minting and transfer of $25 million in institutional mortgage loans on Provenance Blockchain, with each loan minted as an NFT and its data held in a permissioned DataRoom. A patent-pending verification framework lets investors confirm every loan's presence, eligibility, and compliance onchain without accessing borrower data, ahead of a Black Lake vault seeding on NUVA.finance next month.

title: Chainlink Brings Samsung, Toyota and Sony Pricing On-Chain With APAC Equities Streams
blurb: Chainlink launched APAC Equities Streams, an oracle feed putting live on-chain pricing for large Asia-Pacific listings including Samsung, SK Hynix, Toyota, Sony and SoftBank, starting with Japan and Korea. The feeds let developers build equity perps, prediction markets and structured products keyed to Asian companies during local trading hours, with Mainland China, Hong Kong and Taiwan support listed as coming soon.

title: Five Former EF Researchers Launch Ethlabs, an Independent Non-Profit R&D Lab for Ethereum
blurb: Five former Ethereum Foundation researchers, including proposer-builder-separation lead Ansgar Dietrichs and MEV researcher Barnabé Monnot, launched Ethlabs, an independent non-profit R&D lab backing Ethereum as a universal settlement layer. The launch lands amid an EF researcher exodus of at least eight senior departures in 2026, with anchor funding from Bitmine, Sharplink, and Consensys founder Joe Lubin.

title: Andrew Cuomo Tapped to Co-Chair OKX-ICE Crypto Joint Venture
blurb: Andrew Cuomo will co-chair the OKX-ICE crypto joint venture alongside ICE futures executive Trabue Bland, the companies disclosed Monday, marking the highest-profile political appointment yet in ICE's crypto push. The venture, formed alongside ICE's $25B-valuation investment in OKX, will license OKX spot prices for US-regulated crypto futures and route OKX's 120M-plus account holders to ICE and NYSE's planned tokenized-equities market.

title: Bitcoin ETP outflows push rolling one-year flows negative for first time since 2023: K33
blurb: Rolling one-year notional flows across bitcoin investment vehicles fell to -1,176 BTC as of June 18, the first negative reading since November 2023, according to K33. Global bitcoin ETP holdings now sit at 1,466,029 BTC, down 8% from their peak in the largest drawdown on record, though daily outflows have cooled to 625 BTC over the past two weeks from 4,462 BTC in mid-May.

title: SBI Group launches Japan’s first trust bank-backed stablecoin JPYSC
blurb: SBI Group launched JPYSC, Japan's first stablecoin backed by a trust bank, issued through its SBI VC Trade arm and pegged to the yen. Availability is currently limited to SBI VC Trade accounts while the regulatory and tax treatment of the token gets clarified, capping initial reach to the exchange's existing user base.

title: Kalshi sues Illinois, Pritzker over state bill implementing prediction markets regulatory regime
blurb: Kalshi sued Illinois Governor JB Pritzker and state officials over SB3019, which requires prediction market platforms to obtain a state license and imposes a 0.2% charge on digital asset transactions, arguing the rules are preempted by federal CFTC oversight of its event contracts. The suit deepens a federal-state jurisdiction fight in which the CFTC has now sued nine states, including Illinois, to assert exclusive authority over prediction markets.

title: Binance co-CEO remains ‘committed’ to securing EU license after exchange withdraws Greece bid
blurb: Binance withdrew its MiCA license application in Greece and will pursue authorization in another EU member state, with co-CEO Richard Teng saying the exchange remains committed to securing a license in the coming months. Binance faces a July 1 deadline to comply or cease EU operations, having reportedly met resistance from regulators in Ireland, Latvia, and Greece.

title: World Cup boom sends Polymarket volume up 300% while Kalshi sets open interest records
blurb: Polymarket's "Soccer" category booked over $2B in volume during the World Cup's first ten days, a 300% jump that lifted daily average volume from $53M to roughly $220M, while Kalshi's aggregated open interest hit a record $1.16B, up 350% year-to-date. The divergence is notable: Kalshi's CFTC-regulated rails and direct USD on-ramp are surfacing longer-hold institutional positions, putting it in direct competition with DraftKings and FanDuel rather than just Polymarket's US arm.

title: Law enforcement groups warn Clarity Act could hinder crypto crime investigations
blurb: Four U.S. law enforcement organizations sent a joint letter to the DOJ and White House warning that Section 604 of the Clarity Act, which grants non-custodial developers a safe harbor from money-transmitter rules, would create gaps in anti-money-laundering oversight and hinder crypto crime investigations. The pushback, echoed by nearly 100 Catholic leaders citing human-trafficking concerns, adds friction as the bill moves through Congress.

title: Binance Withdraws Greece MiCA Application, Targets New EU Jurisdiction Before July Deadline
blurb: Binance withdrew its MiCA license application in Greece and confirmed it will seek authorization in another EU member state, with seven days left before the bloc's July 1 wind-down deadline for unauthorized crypto firms. Regulators in Greece, Ireland, and Latvia reportedly flagged Binance's past money-laundering penalties and corporate structure, and ESMA referred the Greek application for further review before the exchange pulled it.

title: a16z-Backed Goldfinch Finance Winds Down After Originating $100M in Loans
blurb: Goldfinch Finance, the a16z- and Coinbase Ventures-backed onchain private credit protocol, is winding down via governance proposal GIP-87 after six years and roughly $100M in originated loans, with depositors reporting more than $50M stranded across eight borrowers, two in default and six in restructuring. Warbler Labs will shift Goldfinch into maintenance mode to collect remaining legacy payments, having spent $7M of its own funds repaying lenders amid weak demand for onchain private credit.

title: WSJ: $1.9M in Fake Bets Propped Up Polymarket Creator Videos
blurb: A Wall Street Journal investigation found that roughly $1.9M in bets shown across more than 1,100 creator videos promoting Polymarket were fabricated, with creators filmed placing trades on dummy versions of the platform, including one at the misspelled domain "poiymarket.com." The disclosure lands as Polymarket pursues U.S. re-entry through its CFTC-licensed exchange and courts institutional backing, raising fresh scrutiny of a platform that already settled with the CFTC for $1.4M in 2022.

title: 1,200 Tech Companies Tell Senate to Pass CLARITY Act as Lummis Calls Code-as-Crime Risk an 'Absurdity'
blurb: The Consumer Technology Association, whose 1,200 members include Amazon, Apple and Google, sent a June 17 letter urging Senate leaders Thune and Schumer to bring the CLARITY Act to the floor and pass it without delay. The push marks the fourth constituency letter in three weeks and broadens the bill's backing beyond crypto-native firms, with Senator Cynthia Lummis warning that missing the current window could push market-structure legislation to 2030.

title: Upheaval at the Ethereum Foundation has some of crypto’s biggest names feeling bullish
blurb: The Ethereum Foundation cut its budget by roughly 40% and laid off about 20% of staff, one day after the launch of EthLabs, a new research organization backed by major ecosystem stakeholders. Critics read the cuts as financial stress and a risk to spot-ether ETF flows, while figures including SharpLink CEO Joseph Chalom framed the restructuring as Ethereum maturing into a more institutionally decentralized network.

title: Kalshi targets a massive $40 billion valuation, widening lead over rival Polymarket
blurb: Kalshi is seeking fresh capital at roughly a $40B valuation, nearly double the $22B it raised at previously, with the round potentially closing in Q3, per a Financial Times report. The deal would widen the federally regulated exchange's lead over rival Polymarket, last reported seeking funding at $15B, as Kalshi weighs an IPO no earlier than 2027.

title: Binance withdraws Greek MiCA bid but vows to remain in Europe
blurb: Binance withdrew its application for a MiCA license in Greece and will seek authorization in another EU member state instead, days before a July 1 deadline that requires crypto firms to hold a license in at least one member state or wind down operations across the bloc. Greek, Irish, and Latvian regulators had reportedly flagged concerns over Binance's past legal issues and corporate structure, leaving the exchange's EU access for millions of users unresolved.

title: ENS DAO Opens Temp Check on Handing Treasury and Day-to-Day Authority to ENS Foundation
blurb: The ENS DAO opened a temp-check proposal to transfer treasury control, grants administration, and long-term capital strategy to an expanded five-seat ENS Foundation board, while tokenholders retain protocol-layer authority and the power to remove directors. Material funding decisions would require four of five director votes, and the Foundation would be barred from voting, lending, or delegating any ENS tokens it holds.

title: Dormant Wallet Tied to HashFlare Fraud Moves 10,600 ETH Worth $18.5M
blurb: A wallet tied to the HashFlare cloud-mining fraud moved 10,600 ETH worth roughly $18.5M on Monday, its first activity in about three and a half years, after investigator ZachXBT flagged the transfer. The operator began converting the ether to Bitcoin through cross-chain instant swaps including Near Intents, matching the laundering pattern ZachXBT documented in recent runs like the KelpDAO exploit aftermath.

title: Blockchain data provider Allium raises $40M in funding
blurb: Allium raised $40M in a Series A led by Amplify Partners, with Kleiner Perkins and Theory Ventures participating, following a tenfold revenue increase over the past two years. The company runs a cloud platform that aggregates transaction data from more than 150 blockchains and sells it to banks, government agencies, and fintechs through services including Allium Terminal, Explorer, and the newer AgentHub for AI agents.

title: Stablecoin Issuer KYC Rules Proposed
blurb: US federal regulators including FinCEN, the OCC, the Federal Reserve, the FDIC, and the NCUA proposed rules requiring GENIUS-compliant stablecoin issuers like Circle to run bank-grade Customer Identification Programs, with 24/7 sanctions screening and the ability to freeze or burn flagged tokens. Because the mandate covers only primary on- and off-ramps, Fed Vice Chair Michael Barr warned that secondary-market purchases from pseudonymous addresses could bypass identity checks entirely.

title: Bitcoin miners face deepening margin squeeze as revenue falls below production costs
blurb: Bitcoin miner revenue has fallen below production costs for an estimated 20% of the network, squeezing margins as hashprice declines outpace operators' ability to cut overhead. The stress is also showing up on-chain, where weaker economics pressure smaller miners to power down rigs and concentrate hashrate among lower-cost industrial operations.

title: Trump's refusal to sign housing bill could delay Congress and imperil Clarity Act
blurb: Trump cancelled the signing ceremony for Congress' bipartisan housing bill, which carries a four-year ban on a Federal Reserve CBDC, demanding passage of the unrelated SAVE America voter-ID act first. The delay stalls a policy win for digital-dollar critics and threatens the crypto industry's Clarity Act, the market structure bill that has only weeks left to clear the Senate.

title: Prediction market polls find US voters prefer federal rules over state patchwork
blurb: Two polls commissioned by the Coalition for Prediction Markets found that 48% of Republican and 45% of Democratic voters favor a single federal framework for regulating prediction markets, versus 27% and 35% respectively who back state-by-state oversight. The results land as the CFTC, Kalshi, and Polymarket fight states over jurisdiction, including Kalshi's new suit against Illinois and the CFTC's case against Kentucky.

title: Lummis Sets July as Senate Floor Deadline for Clarity Act, Tells Dimon to Read the Bill
blurb: Senator Cynthia Lummis said the Digital Asset Market Clarity Act will reach the Senate floor by July, the first hard floor-date commitment from the bill's lead sponsor, and dismissed JPMorgan CEO Jamie Dimon's criticism by noting the bill contains over 1,600 references to existing AML and Bank Secrecy Act requirements. The measure needs 60 votes for cloture, requiring at least seven Democratic crossovers from a caucus still divided over ethics language and Section 604's developer-liability carve-out.

title: A16z-Linked Wallet Pulls 25,560 ETH From Binance in Apparent Accumulation Move
blurb: A wallet attributed to Andreessen Horowitz withdrew 25,560 ETH, worth about $42.62M, from Binance on Tuesday, implying an average entry near $1,668 per token, according to onchain tracker Lookonchain. The withdrawal landed with ETH down roughly 26.9% over the past 30 days and trading around $1,560, consistent with a self-custody accumulation posture, though a16z has not confirmed the wallet's ownership.

title: Re7 Labs Opens $223K USDC Compensation Pool for USR Exploit Victims
blurb: Re7 Labs opened a 223,000 USDC compensation pool for users whose positions in its Re7 USDC Base and Re7 USDC Core Mainnet vaults were caught in the March 23 Resolv Labs exploit, with pro-rata claims routed through the Merkl app. The payout follows Gauntlet's larger 4.37 million USDC settlement on June 4, leaving smaller Morpho curators kpk and 9summits as the remaining open files on the cleanup.

title: Podcast: Inside the $1B-a-Day Stablecoin Market Maker for 1,500 Institutions, with B2C2's Cactus Raazi
blurb: B2C2's Cactus Raazi joined Lex's podcast to detail how the institutional market maker prices across 40+ exchanges for roughly 1,500 clients and moves about $1B a day in stablecoin flow. He argues crypto market making splits between riskless price aggregation and proprietary alpha, lays out B2C2's bet on a multi-issuer stablecoin world, and introduces PENNY for instant zero-cost cross-stablecoin swaps.

title: Pump.fun Parent Baton Corporation Recruiting CLO at Up to $5M Base Salary
blurb: Baton Corporation, the UK-based developer behind Pump.fun, is recruiting a Chief Legal Officer at a $1M to $5M base salary, co-founder Alon Cohen posted on X. The hire will sit alongside an existing General Counsel and lead US digital-asset regulatory engagement across the SEC, CFTC, FinCEN and OFAC, plus AML/KYC oversight and cross-border compliance spanning the UK FCA and EU MiCA.

title: Why stablecoins are good for the card networks, not a threat to them
blurb: Argues stablecoins expand card-network volume rather than bypass it, since Visa and Mastercard monetize the settlement layer, not just payment rails.

title: Tempo's machine-to-machine payment transactions are accelerating fast
blurb: Tempo, the payments-focused blockchain backed by Stripe and Paradigm, reported a sharp rise in machine-to-machine payment volume, with autonomous agents and services settling transactions directly on-chain. The trend points to programmatic, API-driven payments becoming an early production use case for the network, ahead of the consumer and merchant flows that typically anchor payment rails.

title: Kraken-incubated Ink upgrades to Optimism’s OP Enterprise Fully Managed in multi-year infrastructure deal
blurb: Ink, a Kraken-incubated Ethereum L2 built on the OP Stack, upgraded to Optimism's OP Enterprise Fully Managed tier under a multi-year deal, handing production infrastructure operations to Optimism while the Ink Foundation focuses on ecosystem growth. As a design partner, Ink will help shape OP Enterprise features including programmable block building, one-day withdrawals to Ethereum, and sequencer-level compliance tooling.

title: Strategy Stock Falls Below $100 for First Time in Two Years as Analysts Pick Apart Its Bitcoin Bet
blurb: Strategy shares fell below $100 on Wednesday for the first time since March 2024, dropping more than 7% to near $98 and pushing its $36B market value below the roughly $50B in Bitcoin it holds. The discount erases the premium-to-NAV that anchored MSTR's leveraged-Bitcoin pitch, and STRC, its 11.5% perpetual preferred, slid to a record-low $81.8, tightening Strategy's ability to issue fresh preferred shares at favorable terms.

title: Digital Chamber CEO Carbone Presses Senate for CLARITY Act Vote, Citing Financial Friction Cost to Americans
blurb: The Digital Chamber CEO Cody Carbone testified before the Senate Banking Committee, pressing for a floor vote on the CLARITY Act and arguing the crypto market-structure bill would cut costs on remittances, merchant payments, and asset transfers for lower-income households. The bill cleared committee 15-9 on May 14 but still needs roughly seven Democratic votes to reach 60 for cloture, with Lummis now eyeing an August-recess vote.

title: ENS DAO Delegates Call Foundation Proposal a Governance Attack as Johnson Self-Delegates
blurb: ENS DAO delegates labeled a temp check from ENS Labs COO Katherine Wu a governance attack, arguing it would hand day-to-day control of the DAO's treasury, ENS token holdings, and Karpatkey-managed Endowment to a five-seat Foundation board.
