Opposition hardened after ENS Labs founder Nick Johnson self-delegated roughly 50% of voting supply, prompting a Security Council member to prepare a veto transaction requiring three additional co-signers.

title: House Sets July CLARITY Act Field Hearing as Lummis Presses for a Senate Floor Vote Before the Recess
blurb: The House Financial Services Committee scheduled a July 17 field hearing in New York on the CLARITY Act as Senator Cynthia Lummis pushed for a Senate floor vote before the August recess. The market-structure bill passed the House 294-134 last year but still needs seven Democratic votes to clear the Senate's 60-vote cloture threshold, with only Gallego and Alsobrooks backing it so far.

title: CFTC Sues Kentucky, Making It the Ninth State in Prediction-Market Jurisdiction Fight
blurb: The CFTC sued Kentucky on Tuesday, bringing to nine the number of states facing federal litigation over jurisdiction of prediction markets like Kalshi and Polymarket. The agency argues the Commodity Exchange Act grants it exclusive authority over event contracts, seeking to block Kentucky from enforcing state gaming statutes and a 14.25% excise tax against CFTC-registered exchanges.

title: CryptoQuant says Michael Saylor's Strategy should halt its bitcoin buying
blurb: CryptoQuant urged Michael Saylor's Strategy to pause its bitcoin purchases and rebuild cash, noting the firm's dividend coverage on STRC preferred stock has fallen from over seven years to about 14 months. STRC trades roughly 17.5% below its $100 par as annual dividend obligations quadrupled to $1.2B, while Strategy sits on a $10.6B unrealized loss on its BTC holdings.

title: Ex-FCA policy insider explains the ‘great divide’ in the UK’s crypto ambition
blurb: A former FCA official now leading global policy at Hedera, Isadora Arredondo argues that the UK's stalled crypto-hub ambitions stem less from regulatory hostility than from a gap between policy design and execution, with institutional and wholesale activity advancing while startups face slow authorization under legacy rules. She contrasts this split approach with the EU's dedicated MiCA framework and points to interoperability across blockchains, stablecoins, and CBDCs as the next phase.

title: Sports-based prediction markets app Onyx Odds raises $20 million in round led by Kraken parent Payward
blurb: Onyx Odds, a sports-focused prediction markets app, raised $20M in a Series A led by Payward, the parent company of crypto exchange Kraken, at a $220M valuation. The deal pulls Kraken into a crowded prediction-markets race against Kalshi, recently valued at $22B, plus Polymarket and exchange entrants Coinbase and Gemini, and includes integration with Payward's B2B infrastructure and in-app crypto trading.

title: Crypto Deal Between Cantor SPAC and Adam Back DAT Gets Delayed
blurb: The vote on a SPAC merger between a Cantor Fitzgerald-linked company and Adam Back's bitcoin-focused digital-asset treasury firm has been delayed, pushing back a closely watched deal in the public-markets bitcoin-treasury trade. The postponement adds uncertainty to a structure that lets investors gain leveraged bitcoin exposure through listed equity rather than holding the asset directly.

title: Senate Democrats Demand Hearings on $500M Trump-UAE-World Liberty Financial Deal
blurb: Five Senate Democrats, led by Elizabeth Warren, sent a letter to Republican committee chairs demanding hearings into a reported $500M deal in which UAE-linked entities bought a 49% stake in World Liberty Financial, the crypto firm tied to President Trump. The senators want officials to testify under oath about $218M paid upfront to entities tied to the Trump and Witkoff families before the January 2025 inauguration.

title: 0x Opens Swap API to AI Agents Paying $0.01 Per Request in USDC
blurb: 0x opened its Swap API to AI agents that pay $0.01 per request in USDC from their own wallets, with no API key, account, or billing setup required. Built with Alchemy AgentPay on the HTTP 402 standard, the integration lets autonomous agents tap 0x's DEX liquidity aggregation on demand, settling payments via x402 on Base and Solana or the Machine Payment Protocol.

title: Circle Publishes Official USDC Spec for Machine Payments Protocol, Enabling Crosschain Agent-to-Agent Commerce
blurb: Circle published a formal USDC method specification for the Machine Payments Protocol, standardizing how AI agents settle USDC payments across EVM chains and Solana via an HTTP 402 challenge-response flow signed with EIP-3009 authorizations. The spec adds the first crosschain payment profile through Circle Gateway and extends coverage to USDCx on Stacks, positioning USDC as a named method type alongside MPP's existing card, Lightning, and Stripe charge specs.

title: Bank of England Publishes Policy Statement and Draft Rules for Systemic Stablecoins
blurb: The Bank of England published a policy statement and draft Code of Practice for systemic sterling stablecoins, replacing proposed per-person holding caps with a £40B issuance ceiling per coin and a backing mix of up to 70% short-dated gilts plus at least 30% unremunerated central bank deposits. HM Treasury still designates which coins qualify as systemic, leaving non-systemic issuers under the FCA, with industry feedback open until September 22.

title: Senate Passes Housing Bill With Fed CBDC Ban Through 2030 in 85-5 Vote
blurb: The US Senate passed the 21st Century ROAD to Housing Act 85-5, sending a statutory ban on a Federal Reserve CBDC through December 31, 2030 toward the president's desk after standalone versions stalled. The text bars direct and indirect Fed issuance of a digital dollar but carves out permissionless, cash-like private stablecoins, shielding existing issuers and the GENIUS Act framework from the prohibition.

title: NYSE Parent ICE and OKX Form 50-50 Joint Venture for Tokenized Equities, Co-Chaired by Andrew Cuomo
blurb: Intercontinental Exchange, the NYSE parent, and crypto exchange OKX are forming a 50-50 joint venture to tokenize NYSE-listed equities, co-chaired by former New York Governor Andrew Cuomo. Pending regulatory approval, the venture will operate as a US registered broker-dealer and futures commission merchant, giving OKX's customers access to ICE futures and tokenized NYSE equities, following ICE's March investment that valued OKX at $25B.

title: Zelle to Launch Stablecoin and Expand to India
blurb: Early Warning Services, the bank consortium behind Zelle, said it will launch ZelleUSD (ZLUSD), a dollar-backed stablecoin, later this year and expand the Zelle app to India without the token. The move puts the seven owner banks, including JPMorgan, BofA, and Wells Fargo, directly into stablecoin issuance, with ZLUSD positioned to power future cross-border remittance corridors like Mexico and Argentina.

title: Congress Passes Fed CBDC Ban Through 2030, Sends Bill to Trump
blurb: Congress passed legislation barring the Federal Reserve from issuing a central bank digital currency through December 31, 2030, attaching the prohibition to the 21st Century ROAD to Housing Act, which cleared the Senate 85-5 and the House 396-13 before heading to Trump for signature. Paired with the GENIUS Act's stablecoin framework, the ban cements Washington's digital-dollar posture as private stablecoins over a state-issued retail token for at least four years.

title: Zuckerberg Orders Meta to Build Standalone Prediction Market App, Codenamed Arena
blurb: Mark Zuckerberg has directed a small internal Meta team to build a standalone prediction-market app codenamed "Arena," initially using a points-based system rather than real-money wagering, putting Meta on a collision course with Polymarket and Kalshi. Meta's 3.56 billion daily active users dwarf every existing prediction-market venue combined, and the news pushed Robinhood and DraftKings shares lower this week.

title: Morgan Stanley Files Amended S-1s for Spot Solana and Ethereum Trusts
blurb: Morgan Stanley filed amended S-1s for spot Solana and Ethereum trusts, naming BNY Mellon and Coinbase Custody as joint custodians and setting a 0.14% sponsor fee with MSOL and MSSE listing on NYSE Arca. The fee matches the bank's bitcoin ETF MSBT and undercuts rival altcoin issuers, though Canary's Litecoin ETF has drawn just $9M in eight months, leaving institutional demand for single-asset altcoin wrappers unproven.

title: Investors get real-time view of UK bond market activity for the first time
blurb: The FCA launched a bond consolidated tape operated by ETS Connect UK, giving investors a single real-time view of UK bond prices and trading activity for the first time, with 98% market coverage. The launch builds on December 2025 transparency rules that lifted real-time reporting of corporate bond trades from under 5% to over 75%, and makes the UK the first market outside North America to consolidate bond data this way.

title: Digital euro clears key hurdle as EU seeks to break free from U.S. credit cards
blurb: The European Parliament's economic committee approved draft rules for a digital euro, advancing the ECB's central-bank-backed electronic wallet that aims to reduce the euro zone's reliance on Visa and Mastercard amid fraying transatlantic relations. Final approval is targeted by year-end ahead of a 12-month pilot in late 2027 and a full launch in 2029, though banks remain wary of deposit outflows and lost interchange revenue.

title: SBI Group Plans to Issue Yen Stablecoin JPYSC as Early as This Week, Nikkei Reports
blurb: SBI Group plans to issue JPYSC, its yen-linked stablecoin, as early as this week after receiving FSA approval, making it the first Japanese financial institution to launch a trust-bank-structured stablecoin under the Payment Services Act. The Type III classification carries no remittance cap, unlike JPYC's fund-transfer tier, opening JPYSC to enterprise treasury settlement and large-volume institutional transactions distributed through SBI VC Trade.

title: Trump lands in Senate's crosshairs over $500 million UAE investment in his crypto venture
blurb: Senate Democrats requested hearings into a $500M investment by UAE officials in World Liberty Financial, the Trump family crypto venture, and whether it shaped administration policy toward the Gulf state. The deal, which closed four days before the inauguration, gave UAE-linked buyers a 49% stake and paid $218M upfront to entities tied to the Trump family and Middle East envoy Steve Witkoff.

title: Who gets a direct line to the Fed? Congress weighs risks of Fed ‘skinny accounts’ for crypto and fintech firms
blurb: The House Financial Services Committee debated the Federal Reserve's proposed "skinny" master accounts, which would give crypto and fintech firms direct access to the Fed's payment rails rather than routing through partner banks. The fight pits a crypto industry calling the change overdue against community banks warning that novel firms escape the compliance regime banks face, with Trump's May executive order already pushing the Fed to evaluate access.

title: SecondFi loses $2.4 million in Cardano wallet exploit
blurb: SecondFi, the Cardano wallet formerly known as Yoroi, confirmed that three separate attacks drained 16 million ADA, roughly $2.4 million, from 374 wallets through a flaw in its proprietary wallet-generation software. The team routed a further 129 million ADA to a third-party custodian before attackers could reach it, though security firm SlowMist estimates total losses could exceed $20 million pending an independent audit.

title: World Liberty Financial's USD1 Supply Grows 9.7% in a Week to $4.85 Billion
blurb: World Liberty Financial's USD1 stablecoin added roughly $427 million over seven days, a 9.7% jump to $4.85 billion that makes it the fourth-largest dollar-pegged token behind Tether, USDC and Sky's USDS. The mint ran counter to USDS and PayPal's PYUSD, which both contracted over the same window, and traces to Aster routing its RWA perpetuals through USD1 and a UFC prize payout, though DefiLlama lists no public audit attestation for the reserves.

title: SBI Group launches Japan’s first trust bank-backed stablecoin JPYSC
blurb: SBI Group launched JPYSC, Japan's first stablecoin backed by a trust bank, issued through its SBI VC Trade subsidiary. Access is restricted to SBI VC Trade accounts until regulators and tax authorities clarify how the yen-pegged token will be treated, limiting near-term reach but establishing a chartered-trust model for fiat-backed issuance in Japan.

title: Trump White House Negotiating CLARITY Act Ethics Deal With Senate Democrats
blurb: A Trump White House official is directly negotiating an ethics compromise on the CLARITY Act with Senate Democrats, the last barrier to a Senate floor vote on the crypto market-structure bill. The dispute centers on language restricting officials' crypto holdings, complicated by the estimated $2.3B Trump and his family have generated from ventures including World Liberty Financial and his namesake memecoin since January.

title: Securitize Wraps Roubini's SEC-Registered ETF as Dubai VARA Digital Security
blurb: Securitize will tokenize economist Nouriel Roubini's SEC-registered Atlas America Fund (Nasdaq: USAF) into USAFi, a digital security issued under Dubai VARA's asset-referenced rulebook and custodied at BNY, targeting a Q3 2026 launch. The structure lets the ETF trade permissionlessly with 24/7 settlement, giving institutional users outside the US portable, regulated collateral for DeFi rails alongside Securitize's existing BlackRock BUIDL and Hamilton Lane mandates.

title: Ripple Wins Preliminary MiCA CASP Approval in Luxembourg, Unlocking EEA Passporting
blurb: Ripple received preliminary CASP approval from Luxembourg's CSSF under MiCA, a conditional green light that, once final conditions are met, lets it passport its payments platform across all 30 EEA member states without separate national filings. Paired with Ripple's existing EU EMI license, the combined authorizations will give European banks and fintechs single-integration access to its collect, exchange, and pay-out stack and make the firm fully MiCA-compliant.

title: Franklin Templeton Closes 250 Digital Acquisition, Launches Franklin Crypto for Institutional Allocators
blurb: Franklin Templeton closed its acquisition of 250 Digital and launched Franklin Crypto, an active digital-asset unit targeting pensions, sovereign wealth funds, and other institutional allocators, with part of the consideration paid in its on-chain BENJI tokens. The move gives the $1.78T manager three institutional crypto lines, layering active management onto its existing tokenized-treasury exposure, which has crossed $2.5B in AUM and roughly doubled year to date.

title: Binance says it will make a fresh push for permission to operate in the EU after its MiCA license application in Greece failed ahead of the June 30 deadline (Reuters)
blurb: Binance will renew its bid for a MiCA license to operate across the EU after its application in Greece failed ahead of the June 30 compliance deadline. The setback leaves the largest crypto exchange without passportable authorization in the bloc, ceding regulated EU ground to rivals like Coinbase and Kraken that already hold MiCA approval.

title: Black Lake, Nuva Labs tokenize $25 million in mortgage loans on Provenance in RWA push
blurb: Black Lake Digital Markets and Nuva Labs completed what they call the first onchain minting and transfer of $25M in institutional mortgage loans on Provenance Blockchain, minting each loan as an NFT with data held in a permissioned DataRoom. A patent-pending verification framework lets investors confirm every loan's presence, eligibility, and origination compliance onchain without touching borrower data, with the tranche set to seed a Black Lake vault on NUVA.finance next month.

title: Meta Has Created a Prediction Markets App
blurb: Meta is building an experimental prediction markets app internally called Arena, kept independent of Facebook and Instagram. The app would put Meta in direct competition with Polymarket and Kalshi, the two largest prediction-market platforms, as event-contract trading draws growing mainstream and regulatory attention.

title: Coinbase's Tokenized Stocks With 'True Equity Ownership'
blurb: Coinbase announced tokenized stocks that it markets as conferring "true equity ownership," but it has not disclosed the underlying legal structure that determines whether holders own real shares or a derivative claim. That structure governs the regulatory treatment, user redemption rights, and competitive positioning against rivals like Robinhood and Backed, making the missing detail the central question for anyone evaluating the product.

title: Big Banks Pass Fed Stress Test, Quickly Boost Payouts
blurb: All 33 of the largest US banks cleared the Federal Reserve's annual stress test, clearing the way for lenders to raise buybacks and dividends as soon as the results posted. The strong showing reflects a stress scenario the Fed softened this year, lowering the capital cushion banks must hold and freeing up billions for shareholder payouts.

title: Cboe launches prediction market suite with binary option contracts on S&P 500
blurb: Cboe launched a prediction market suite featuring binary option contracts tied to the S&P 500, giving traders regulated exposure to event-based outcomes on the index. The contracts are live on Interactive Brokers and slated to reach Charles Schwab in the coming months, pushing prediction markets further into mainstream brokerage rails.

title: Andrew Cuomo Tapped to Co-Chair OKX-ICE Crypto Joint Venture
blurb: OKX and Intercontinental Exchange named former New York governor Andrew Cuomo to co-chair their crypto joint venture, alongside ICE futures executive Trabue Bland. The venture, formed with ICE's $25B-valuation investment in OKX, will license OKX's spot prices for US-regulated crypto futures and route OKX's 120M-plus account holders to ICE's futures and NYSE's planned tokenized-equities market, pending regulatory approval.

title: Federal Reserve says U.S. banks can withstand $708 billion in losses amid overhaul of capital rules
blurb: The Federal Reserve's annual stress test found all 32 large banks would stay above minimum capital requirements while absorbing more than $708 billion in losses under a severe recession scenario, with projected losses led by $200 billion in credit cards and $160 billion in C&I loans. The results carry no regulatory weight this year, as the Fed froze stress-test buffers until 2027 while reworking its methodology, leaving banks focused on the pending Basel III Endgame proposal instead.

title: Binance co-CEO remains ‘committed’ to securing EU license after exchange withdraws Greece bid
blurb: Binance withdrew its MiCA license application in Greece and will refile in another EU member state, with co-CEO Richard Teng saying the exchange remains committed to authorization in the coming months despite a July 1 deadline. Reuters reported Binance has faced regulatory resistance in Ireland, Latvia, and Greece, meaning the world's largest crypto exchange could be forced to halt EU operations if it fails to secure a license within a week.

title: Kalshi sues Illinois, Pritzker over state bill implementing prediction markets regulatory regime
blurb: Kalshi sued Illinois Governor JB Pritzker and Attorney General Kwame Raoul in federal court over SB3019, which requires prediction-market platforms to obtain a state license and imposes a 0.2% charge on digital-asset transactions, arguing the rules are preempted by CFTC jurisdiction. The suit deepens the federal-versus-state fight over who regulates event contracts, with the CFTC having already sued nine states, including Illinois, to assert exclusive authority before the law takes effect July 1.

title: World Cup boom sends Polymarket volume up 300% while Kalshi sets open interest records
blurb: Polymarket's soccer category cleared over $2B in volume during the World Cup's first ten days, a 300% jump, while Kalshi's aggregated open interest hit a record $1.16B, its first time above the billion mark. The divergence matters: Kalshi's OI is compounding faster than its volume, signaling longer-held directional positions from US institutional and high-net-worth dollar investors drawn to its CFTC-regulated rails and direct USD on-ramp.

title: Law enforcement groups warn Clarity Act could hinder crypto crime investigations
blurb: Four U.S. law enforcement groups, including the National District Attorneys Association and National Sheriffs' Association, sent a joint letter to the DOJ and White House warning that Section 604 of the Clarity Act would shield non-custodial developers as non-money-transmitters and weaken AML oversight. The carveout, originally the standalone Blockchain Regulatory Certainty Act, has also drawn criticism from Catholic leaders citing human-trafficking risks, adding pressure as the bill advances.

title: Binance Withdraws Greece MiCA Application, Targets New EU Jurisdiction Before July Deadline
blurb: Binance withdrew its MiCA license application in Greece and will pursue authorization in an unnamed EU member state, seven days before the bloc's July 1 transitional deadline for unauthorized crypto firms. ESMA flagged the Greek application for further review, and regulators in Ireland and Latvia reportedly raised concerns over Binance's past money-laundering penalties, leaving the exchange racing to secure a charter before enforcement begins.

title: MSUSD drops 85% as proof-of-reserves provider terminates agreement
blurb: MSUSD, a yield token tied to MainStreet Finance, fell as much as 85% after Accountable terminated its proof-of-reserves verification agreement, pushing the Morpho msY/USDC lending market to 100% utilization. MainStreet says assets remain fully backed and blames a shuttered third-party dashboard rather than insolvency, citing over $8M USDC deployed for liquidity, but the depeg already exposed AlphaUSDC Delta V2 to roughly $18M in the affected market.

title: Kalshi targets a massive $40 billion valuation, widening lead over rival Polymarket
blurb: Kalshi is raising fresh capital at roughly a $40B valuation, nearly double the $22B it commanded last round, with the deal potentially closing in Q3, per a Financial Times report. The raise would widen the federally regulated exchange's lead over Polymarket, last seeking funding at $15B, as Kalshi's CEO weighs an IPO no earlier than 2027.

title: Blockchain data provider Allium raises $40M in funding
blurb: Allium raised $40M in a Series A led by Amplify Partners, with Kleiner Perkins and Theory Ventures participating, after more than 10x revenue growth over the past two years. The startup aggregates transaction data from over 150 blockchains and sells it to banks, government agencies, and fintechs, positioning itself as the compliance and analytics data layer as institutions move deeper into crypto markets.

title: Binance withdraws Greek MiCA bid but vows to remain in Europe
blurb: Binance withdrew its MiCA license application in Greece after regulators in Greece, Ireland, and Latvia reportedly flagged its past legal issues and corporate structure, and will now seek authorization in another EU member state. The exchange faces a July 1 MiCA deadline to hold a license in at least one of the 27 member states or wind down operations serving millions of European users.

title: Lama AI raises $10M to accelerate automated loan originations
blurb: Lama AI raised a $10M Series A led by EJF Ventures, bringing total funding past $20M after tripling revenue in the past year, to scale its AI-native loan origination platform for community and regional banks. The platform uses autonomous agents to run intake, document collection, underwriting, and approvals, targeting small-business and government-guaranteed loans where per-loan underwriting costs make the economics hard for smaller lenders.

title: Podcast: Inside the $1B-a-Day Stablecoin Market Maker for 1,500 Institutions, with B2C2's Cactus Raazi
blurb: In this podcast, B2C2's Cactus Raazi explains how the institutional digital-asset market maker prices for roughly 1,500 clients across 40+ exchanges and underpins about $1B a day in stablecoin flow. He argues that market makers aren't interchangeable, with riskless price aggregation and proprietary alpha shops producing very different execution, and lays out B2C2's bet on a multi-issuer stablecoin world and its PENNY product for instant zero-cost cross-stablecoin swaps.

title: Why Europe is rebuilding its tech stack
blurb: A Sifted panel argued that Europe's push to build a sovereign tech stack shifted from policy debate to operational priority after the Trump administration ordered Anthropic to block foreign nationals from its most powerful AI models, a live demonstration of the "digital kill switch." With a Proton survey finding 74% of listed European companies rely on US email providers, CIOs are now under board pressure to cut foreign-provider dependence within one to two years.

title: Rebuilding Securities Finance on Aave V4
blurb: Aave founder Stani Kulechov argues securities finance, a massive Wall Street market, is moving onchain via lending against securities collateral on Aave V4.

title: Meta's WhatsApp head to step down, will be replaced by Indian fintech founder Kunal Shah
blurb: Meta named Cred founder Kunal Shah to lead WhatsApp, replacing seven-year head Will Cathcart, and is investing $900M in his Indian fintech startup at a $4.5B post-money valuation. The appointment ties a credit-card payments operator to a 3-billion-user messaging platform that just began testing subscriptions, signaling Meta wants payments and commerce native to WhatsApp beyond ads.

title: Trump's refusal to sign housing bill could delay Congress and imperil Clarity Act
blurb: Trump cancelled the signing ceremony for Congress's bipartisan housing bill, demanding passage of the unrelated SAVE America Act voter-ID legislation first and pausing the four-year Federal Reserve CBDC ban tucked into the package. The delay threatens the crypto industry's top priority, the Clarity Act market-structure bill, which has only a few weeks left to clear the Senate before its window closes.

title: Alan Greenspan has died
blurb: Alan Greenspan, who chaired the Federal Reserve from 1987 to 2006 and shaped US monetary policy across four presidencies, died at 100. His nineteen-year tenure spanned the 1987 crash, the dot-com boom, and the low-rate era that critics later tied to the 2008 financial crisis, leaving a legacy that still frames how central bankers weigh deregulation against systemic risk.

title: Why stablecoins are good for the card networks, not a threat to them
blurb: The author argues stablecoins help Visa and Mastercard rather than disintermediate them, since buyers rarely bypass cards to pay merchants directly.

title: SPAC Deals Seen as Key Path to Public Market in Data Center Boom
blurb: SPACs have re-emerged as a viable path to public markets for companies building out AI data centers, according to dealmaking veteran Betsy Cohen. The blank-check rebound gives capital-intensive infrastructure firms a faster listing route than traditional IPOs, channeling investor demand for AI exposure into the power, cooling, and compute buildout underpinning the boom.

title: Prediction market polls find US voters prefer federal rules over state patchwork
blurb: Polls commissioned by the Coalition for Prediction Markets found that pluralities of both Republican (48%) and Democratic (45%) voters prefer a single federal framework for regulating prediction markets over a state-by-state approach, with just 8% saying the platforms should be illegal. The findings land as the CFTC, Kalshi, and Polymarket battle states over jurisdiction, including Kalshi's new suit against Illinois and the CFTC's countersuit against Kentucky.

title: JPMorgan Chase unveils $50 billion buyback, Goldman Sachs raises dividend after Fed stress test
blurb: JPMorgan authorized a $50B buyback and a 10% dividend increase to $1.65 per share after the Fed's annual stress test cleared all 32 large banks, with Goldman Sachs, Wells Fargo, and Morgan Stanley also raising payouts. The Fed froze stress capital buffers through 2027 pending a methodology overhaul, so the results carry no capital impact and investors are watching the pending Basel III Endgame proposal instead.

title: Alphabet’s Dow Debut Shows Index Headache in Tech-Driven Economy
blurb: Alphabet joined the Dow Jones Industrial Average, the latest reshuffle in the price-weighted benchmark's effort to keep pace with a market increasingly dominated by Big Tech. The addition underscores a structural problem for the 130-year-old index: its price-weighting methodology distorts the influence of high-priced stocks and leaves it perpetually lagging the tech-heavy, market-cap-weighted S&P 500 that most institutional capital actually tracks.
